title: Drosera tokaiensis
taxonomy: Drosera tokaiensis belongs to the family Droseraceae, commonly known as the sundew family. This carnivorous plant is classified in the genus Drosera, which includes approximately 194 species of sundews. The classification of Drosera tokaiensis places it within the order Ericales, which comprises flowering plants that produce berries or capsules. Furthermore, Drosera tokaiensis is classified in the class Magnoliopsida, which encompasses dicotyledonous plants. At the kingdom level, Drosera tokaiensis is classified as Plantae, indicating its status as a vascular plant.

intro: Drosera tokaiensis, also known as Tokai sundew, is a carnivorous plant species that belongs to the Droseraceae family. The health benefits of this plant include its potential in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of Drosera tokaiensis include flavonoids, terpenes, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Drosera tokaiensis include tinctures, capsules, and syrups, often used in traditional herbal medicine to alleviate respiratory issues and coughs.
